description: Manage access to every application and web account for every member. We can securely share passwords, secrets and more.
author: Colin C.
pros: This software is so much more than a password manager. Offers security and productivity wrapped into one.
cons: It is challenging to use on phone especially with the auto-fill option while signing in.

review_title: Great on-premise password solution for offline cloud environments
description: Overall, great - does what it needs to. More features than some competitors, and the self hosted nature makes it very good for my on-premise environments - definitely feel as if it's more secure than cloud/online based alternatives.
author: Sam W.
pros: Open source - incredibly important to me, I want full control over the security, as well as knowing how it's setup. As long as your setup provides depth security you will be fine - very easy to setup but would prefer more intuitive deployments.
cons: Sometimes, especially with passwords, you want peace of mind - although with self hosted applications you want lots of backups - I wish this was easier, and had the ability to automate it to a number of locations knowing it will restore 100% of the time.

review_title: Going to start it to solve GDRP needs. Installation is a little bit tricky but overall quality OK
author: Francisco M.
pros: Good installation check feature. Good level of security using the browser add on. Documentations is fair.
cons: Installation procedure is a little bit complicated, then is easier to use more time that needed in order to have server up & running
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A nice password manager for teams
description: Although we faced some problems during its deployment, the tool works fine once configured.
author: Sergio G.
pros: - It's open source, which is always nice. - You can share passwords with specific users or groups. - The information is end-to-end encrypted. - You can use MFA with it.
cons: - It's self-hosted, which could be a pro instead a con, but being self-hosted you have to handle updates and security patches. - Some of the configurations (specially the one which is needed to be done in Active Directory) is not very intuitive or simple.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: The best password manager for cohesive collaborating teams
description: With the rapid digitisation of our business, Passbolt has made significant efforts to enforce end to end encryption on data both in storage and on transit. It has also doubled up as our favourite password manager .
author: Pereira S.
pros: In our diverse and agile environment, Passbolt functionality has perfectly suited our teams and data protection daily needs and requirements. Especially now that our business has rapidly moved to digital landscapes, we are grateful to have found a single tool that provide security and data privacy requirements. Passbolt as centralised , organised and enable us to share our credentials which enables us to collaborate securely. Passbolt is highly versatile and protects both on premises and cloud native resources. It has included robust authentication and encryption measures that operate even without internet access. This software uses privacy by design and provide standard security which we have adjusted the settings to match our requirements and risk exposure
cons: In as much as making it so difficult to switch between accounts on web browsers and desktop apps is a security measures it is limiting to us with a very diverse data app. Again the desktop apps is only functional in our Windows powered devices.

review_title: The Ultimate Password Manager of All Times: Passbolt
author: Xiluva M.
pros: Availability of self-hosted and fully-hosted makes it flexible when managing passwords in one place. It offers 2FA and and asymmetric end-to-end encryption to secure passwords for company accounts.
cons: It’s been over 8 months but the only flaw on Passbolt is pricing which is a bit high compared to alternatives such as Zoho Vault.

review_title: Most affordable open-source password manager for the businesses !
description: The best in class security features at most affordable pricing is quite attractive for users and that's why mostly preferred amongst all business teams. The self hosting feature of Passbolt is quite impressive and attracts users for password management start to end. A must buy for all the businesses.
author: Anurag Kumar S.
pros: Passbolt is every professional's choice for secure and robust password management. The software is embedded with high end security features which allows advance encryption for managing business security end to end. It is one of the best open source password management software with easy and secure sharing of passwords amongst different teams for improved security and productivity of any business.
cons: The initial set up is bit complex and often pose some issue to users. The users need a detailed training on technical aspects of the software before final deployment of the software in ground. The self hosting feature should be carefully deployed and integration aspect should be made more smooth and effortless.

review_title: It integrates efficiently with the browser to ensure real layers of data security
author: Romi D.
pros: Passbolt I liked it very much, I can save all my credentials on it and access them securely. With Passbolt the encryption is very strong and effective against any threats. I liked the collaborative features of Passbolt, it creates a secure environment for sharing sensitive data.
cons: I have not found anything simpler than Passbolt, it is very easy to install on any browser without complications.

review_title: Good security increases shareholder value, passbolt is the missing key.
description: A satisfied user and I devote all my loyalty to Passbolt. It's a game changer.
author: Walter K.
pros: Passbolt offers well detailed data breach reports and it also has an emergency access options. Passbolt is what we need for smooth password capture and replay. I have used it long enough to say that it's the best in class of security. Job well done passbolt!
cons: Passbolt's top priority is security and their customer service is always on top. No concerns raised so far.

author: Ibrahim L.
pros: Passbolt is highly customizable and is an open-source password manager which allows me to generate, store and share passwords in real-time.
cons: I acquire everything I yearn for when using Passbolt - no issues detected so far.

author: Maxence L.
pros: Passbolt login system is the most trusted in the market. From dashboard to authorisation, it is extremely foolproof.
cons: Satisfied with the way it handles the login
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Kyle J.
pros: Passbolt is a great password managment tool and it's free and open source. It securely stores and shares passwords with team members which makes sharing account easy and secure. Not more separate storage of password or having to remind other co-workers of passwords.
cons: No cons from me. Great all around free password management tool. Makes life easier and more secure.

description: It is easy to share and manage passwords with teams in real-time.
author: Tonie M.
pros: Passbolt is full open-source which makes it super easy to customize without coding. In addition, it has collaboration capability which allows users to share passwords in one place.
cons: Fully hosted enterprise tier is a bit expensive to upgrade compared to alternatives.

author: Susan G.
pros: Passbolt allows us (teams) to securely collaborate on passwords in one place. Availability of two factor authentication enables me to encrypt passwords in real-time.
cons: I slightly dislike that Passbolt only store passwords and dies not support support for other data such as credit card details.

review_title: Brainstorming your ideas the right way with Passbolt, best in class of security.
description: Very versatile easy to install and easy to manage. A unique and worth experience from Passbolt Kudos!
author: Peter N K.
pros: Finally I am happy to announce to you all that we have a password software which security is their key agenda. Passbolt is flexible and advanced for security purposes. Stay in control of your data. It never fails.
cons: No major challenges but it's doings are highly noticable.

description: Passwords can be shared team wise and it’s very safe to keep our credentials away from threats.
author: Kabir A.
pros: With it we can easily save our credentials and access them when in need via online browser. Has amazing customer support to its users.
cons: Passbolt is a must have tool , nothing to dislike.

author: Luca L.
pros: With Passbolt, password management is simplified in a corporate cooperation environment. It is very useful because you can create users and groups and assign different passwords to them. This way, it's possible to store both corporate and personal passwords.
cons: Probably the installation and configuration. I'm stuck with .deb package not installing on Ubuntu (for my fault in settings) and i can't proceed

author: Alfred V.
pros: The infrastructure provided by Passbolt is perfect for auto generation of passwords. Ease of use is considered for users.
cons: Doesn't hang a lot while utilizing its features.

author: Beau B.
pros: I like the fact that Passbolt sends me text alert whenever there is an access to my account. This ensures that if someone is trying to access then I can be notified immediately.
cons: Text alerts have always arrived on time.

author: Adriano M.
pros: I like how simple it is to verify myself to access the remote desktop. It also acts as a barrier from suspicious access.
cons: I got no concerns with regards to this tool.

author: Paola V.
pros: Passbolt enables me to encrypt passwords in one central repository.
cons: Never have I come across even a single flaw on Passbolt.

review_title: Passbolt, another way to circle around about passwords managers onboarding gap's
description: Passbolt is relatively new for us, but the way it can act both proactively and prompt free help us satisfying all users needs, from advanced to low it background one's, and help us avoid rejection on password manager use.
author: Jonathan D.
pros: The full feature set available, including TOPs and Teams management for free, a game changer for us.
cons: Some part of the admin center could be more using friendly, needing less clicks to manage actions, like groups membership and linking users to groups.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A complete and worthwhile password manager
description: I am very happy with Passbolt for its ease of configuration and use. In addition, the team is very friendly and in the community forum they listen to suggestions to add to the platform and help you if you have any problems. It is not an airtight system of which you know nothing about the equipment or how it is made, since it is open source and you can contact the team or the community for any problem
author: Diego S.
pros: I like your workflow, making sure that the passwords are secure by keeping them encrypted until the end and only decrypting them on the client. Also its integrations and features that make it easy to use and are quick to configure using its guides. It's definitely worth using it even if you don't pay for the subscription and use the free version
cons: If I had to look for a catch, perhaps it would be not being able to share passwords with other users of other servers or that you need to have an internet connection to access. But to be honest, these are problems that all password managers have and without internet you probably don't even need to access the sites so I wouldn't know how to look for something bad since I love it

author: Shalini A.
pros: I am using this solution for password management. Easy to share credentials with the other team member with full of security. It provider high grade and end to end encryption that protects our password from hacks. Open source solution that makes this awesome.
cons: No cons for me. I am happy with the functionality that it offers.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Password manager with a focus on teams
description: We've been using Passbolt since the early days and like the solution in general. The team-focused features are perfect for sharing passwords in a team. The Passbolt support responds fast and is very focused on solving the raised issues.
author: Stephan H.
pros: Single Sign On (SSO) with Microsoft, Account recovery and the activity log are essential features to be able to use Passbolt in larger teams. Besides the browser plugin, there's also a mobile app and a Windows Desktop app for Passbolt.
cons: More flexibility in storing credentials would be nice. Currently you can only store username and password combinations, sometimes you need to store additional information. You can store that in the description field but that's not ideal.
